title: Avenula agropyroides
intro: Avenula agropyroides, also known as meadow oat-grass, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and urinary tract issues. The bioactive constituents of Avenula agropyroides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Avenula agropyroides is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being included in herbal tonics and infusions to support overall health and well-being.
